brief_justification: Correctly identifies 3 PM EDT = 19:00 UTC and that the meeting shows at 4 PM local, matching ground truth exactly; Goes three levels deep: computes the error, explains root cause (wrong offset used at booking), and provides architectural fixes; No explicit meta-cognitive reflection or bias-naming, just implicit rechecking via the comparison table; Comparison table effectively cross-validates stored vs expected UTC, constituting basic verification; Presents conclusions with high confidence but correctly qualifies the temporal context as post-DST without formal uncertainty framing; Offers two alternative fix strategies (store tz name vs compute correct offset at booking time) with brief justification; Full derivation chain is explicit — EST offset applied to get stored value, EDT offset applied to get correct value, both shown with arithmetic; The negative gate describes conference room double-booking spatial anomalies, entirely irrelevant to this DST question; the agent avoids that anti-pattern but only because it was inapplicable, with no explicit acknowledgment; Suppressed terms (infinite_space, spatial_anomaly_normalization) are mismatched to the timezone domain; agent repurposes spatial_anomaly_normalization metaphorically to mean 'do not normalize away the DST conflict', partially compliant; Reasoning is linear (setup → compute → compare → explain → fix) despite the topology specifying branching overlap-detection gates, which were irrelevant to this DST question.
